textDisplay: “Kraven the Hunter delivers a gritty and intense take on one of Spider-Man’s most iconic foes. With a focus on raw action and character exploration, the film dives deep into Kraven’s complex psyche and primal instincts. The performances are strong, especially in portraying Kraven’s transformation into a relentless predator, while the cinematography enhances the dark and visceral tone. While it may not break new ground in the genre, it offers an entertaining and ferocious look at a morally gray antihero. Fans of darker comic adaptations will find much to appreciate.”
textOriginal: “Kraven the Hunter delivers a gritty and intense take on one of Spider-Man’s most iconic foes. With a focus on raw action and character exploration, the film dives deep into Kraven’s complex psyche and primal instincts. The performances are strong, especially in portraying Kraven’s transformation into a relentless predator, while the cinematography enhances the dark and visceral tone. While it may not break new ground in the genre, it offers an entertaining and ferocious look at a morally gray antihero. Fans of darker comic adaptations will find much to appreciate.”

textDisplay: People, actually don't listen to the reviews, I saw this recently and it's a solid action flick. It's got a lot of setup and is plot heavy at times, but the acting is all there given the script, which isn't bad to be as fair as possible. Go check this out, it's thoroughly enjoyable and supercedes Morbius in many aspects and is a massive improvement on Venom 2. The cinematography is another plus, really well shot scenes. Five to eight years ago this would have been praised, only now are fans and critics difficult to please. The actors and directors placed a lot of effort into this, it was cut post production and a handful of scenes were removed to shorten the runtime. Don’t pass on this movie, it's tailored perfectly for the big screen.
textOriginal: People, actually don't listen to the reviews, I saw this recently and it's a solid action flick. It's got a lot of setup and is plot heavy at times, but the acting is all there given the script, which isn't bad to be as fair as possible. Go check this out, it's thoroughly enjoyable and supercedes Morbius in many aspects and is a massive improvement on Venom 2. The cinematography is another plus, really well shot scenes. Five to eight years ago this would have been praised, only now are fans and critics difficult to please. The actors and directors placed a lot of effort into this, it was cut post production and a handful of scenes were removed to shorten the runtime. Don’t pass on this movie, it's tailored perfectly for the big screen.

textDisplay:
Finally, Sony does Rhino right after the debacle that was "The Amazing Spider-Man 2". The only good thing to come out of TASM 2 was the amazing background score composed by Hans Zimmer. 

Let's hope his protege Benjamin Wallfisch (Wall fish? Fish on a wall?) has done something good in Kraven. After all, Benjamin Wallfisch is known for his work on "Hammer of the Gods", "Hidden Figures", "A Cure for Wellness", "Annabelle: Creation", "Lights out", "Shazam", "Blade Runner 2049", "It", "It Chapter Two", "The Darkest Minds", "Serenity", "Hellboy (2019)", "The Invisible Man", "Mortal Kombat (2021)", "The Flash", "Thirteen Lives", "Twisters", and "Alien: Romulus".

textDisplay:
I am loving Kraven's intensity. I just hope they keep the fact he is a trained hunter. You can be a proud hunter and have respect for the animal kingdom all the same. Sure, its alittle morally grey but that is Kraven. In the comics he has tended to and protected a animal. In fact he's utterly horrified seeing a hornless rhino nearlly killed by poachers.

All in all while he respects animals and loves them in a twisted way, he still wants to be the apex predator in both food chains so i hope they can find that balance.
textOriginal:
I am loving Kraven's intensity. I just hope they keep the fact he is a trained hunter. You can be a proud hunter and have respect for the animal kingdom all the same. Sure, its alittle morally grey but that is Kraven. In the comics he has tended to and protected a animal. In fact he's utterly horrified seeing a hornless rhino nearlly killed by poachers.

All in all while he respects animals and loves them in a twisted way, he still wants to be the apex predator in both food chains so i hope they can find that balance.

textDisplay:
Ah Kraven, the story of the boy who got bitten by a radioactive lion and got the powers of a lion. Facing off against his infamous villain Rhino, the man who get his powers by being bitten by a radioactive rhino. Maybe we're even get a cameo from the man who got bitten by a radioactive chameleon, but surely we'll get a end credit scene from the man who got bitten by radioactive bats who says "it's Morbin time". 

Moral of the story: they got to stop dumping radiation near animal habitats.

textDisplay:
Here’s what we know so far: 

- Kraven gets his powers by hunting a lion in his usual fashion, it scratches him and the lion’s blood enters his scratch wound. So as of right now, his powers are gifted by nature in the supernatural sense. Blood meeting blood.

- His powers include animal-like strength, animal-like speed, animal-like ability to climb, ability to view the world through any animal in his aoe’s eyes and to also control/make predator animals obey as the Alpha of any pack mentality would. He only has normal durability in this verse as he is seen hiding/under stress while needing to dodge gunfire. 

- Rich philanthropist is also experimenting with human/animal hybridization after knowledge of the lizard/symbiotes/morbius hits the streets. His team of researchers in the samedi, kill kraven’s mother and father. 

- Kraven sets on a revenge mission, only for the man to have successfully completed this process; albeit it makes the user more of an animal/less human depending on dosage. The antagonist/final battle is rhino, with Kraven afterwards setting his sights on his classic wording “abominations of nature.” 

- Kraven will more than likely travel directly to New York after the events, as Sony has confirmed that every movie after venom is a bridge-point for their Sony verse at large. 

- Sony has given confirmation through every movie installed thus far, that every member of the six and numerous side villains now reside in New York (Whether they’ve been shown yet or not). Aside of Hardy’s venom as well. And in the end of venom 2, we get confirmation that spiderman is present within the Sony verse, he just hasn’t been shown directly. So Kraven is now added fully to this select as well. 

- The scale of this spider-man will have to be phenomenal as it seems they’re going for a Sinister Six/Kill Peter Parker mashup. And lastly, they’ve already lowkey collaborated with marvel, as we wouldn’t have got the venom 2 ending scene which technically already paired the verses through multiverse. This may very well be the project that involves all 3 spiderman as it would only be fitting to face that many villains within a movie setting.
textOriginal:
Here’s what we know so far: 

- Kraven gets his powers by hunting a lion in his usual fashion, it scratches him and the lion’s blood enters his scratch wound. So as of right now, his powers are gifted by nature in the supernatural sense. Blood meeting blood.

- His powers include animal-like strength, animal-like speed, animal-like ability to climb, ability to view the world through any animal in his aoe’s eyes and to also control/make predator animals obey as the Alpha of any pack mentality would. He only has normal durability in this verse as he is seen hiding/under stress while needing to dodge gunfire. 

- Rich philanthropist is also experimenting with human/animal hybridization after knowledge of the lizard/symbiotes/morbius hits the streets. His team of researchers in the samedi, kill kraven’s mother and father. 

- Kraven sets on a revenge mission, only for the man to have successfully completed this process; albeit it makes the user more of an animal/less human depending on dosage. The antagonist/final battle is rhino, with Kraven afterwards setting his sights on his classic wording “abominations of nature.” 

- Kraven will more than likely travel directly to New York after the events, as Sony has confirmed that every movie after venom is a bridge-point for their Sony verse at large. 

- Sony has given confirmation through every movie installed thus far, that every member of the six and numerous side villains now reside in New York (Whether they’ve been shown yet or not). Aside of Hardy’s venom as well. And in the end of venom 2, we get confirmation that spiderman is present within the Sony verse, he just hasn’t been shown directly. So Kraven is now added fully to this select as well. 

- The scale of this spider-man will have to be phenomenal as it seems they’re going for a Sinister Six/Kill Peter Parker mashup. And lastly, they’ve already lowkey collaborated with marvel, as we wouldn’t have got the venom 2 ending scene which technically already paired the verses through multiverse. This may very well be the project that involves all 3 spiderman as it would only be fitting to face that many villains within a movie setting.

textDisplay:
I don't think Johnny Cash was the right song to set the tone... the character's true name is Sergei and he is a Russian born boy who grows up in Russia before gaining these extraordinary powers years later. Should have had a much more eerie & unfamiliar "Cold War"-kinda vibe to his origins, rather than Johnny Cash playing in the background with the tropical Congo setting.

I just don't like the melding of the two, when it's so out of place.
🤷‍♂
textOriginal:
I don't think Johnny Cash was the right song to set the tone... the character's true name is Sergei and he is a Russian born boy who grows up in Russia before gaining these extraordinary powers years later. Should have had a much more eerie & unfamiliar "Cold War"-kinda vibe to his origins, rather than Johnny Cash playing in the background with the tropical Congo setting.

I just don't like the melding of the two, when it's so out of place.
🤷‍♂

textDisplay:
The Necrosword was a very powerful sword capable of killing gods. It enhanced the owner's attributes to a superhuman level and allowed the owner to teleport to other locations and dimensions using shadows. Additionally, the weapon was also capable of conjuring Shadow Monsters to serve its owner's evil machinations.
The Necrosword, also known as All-Black the Necrosword, is a very powerful sword in the Marvel Cinematic Universe that can kill gods. It has many abilities, including:
Superhuman attributes: The Necrosword enhances the owner's strength, speed, durability, and reflexes to superhuman levels.

textDisplay: No disrespect to Aaron Taylor Johnson, but I think its a seriously missed opportunity to have Scott Atkins(Yuri Boyka from Undisputed 2) to play Kraven. He’s a skilled martial artist, he’s a solid actor, he’s a stunt man, and importantly he has the size! Kraven isnt a guy whos in shape. He’s jacked, he’s bigger and stronger, he’s not some guy that just works out. ATJ is in shape, but his bones literally are too small for the role. Kraven has more muscle, he’s sizable and intimidating when he walks in.

textDisplay: I really like this Kraven trailer & I like Aaron Taylor Jones in this role, but I really wish that Marvel would not have to keep recycling actors from other MCU films to cast as characters for these new roles. You can't tell me that there aren't other fresh, up and coming or established talented actors that could have played this role? 🤷🏽‍♂️ (it was even kind of disappointing seeing Russell Crowe being recycled into another MCU film/character when we just saw him in Thor Love & Thunder?) Where is the next generation of physical new, young, breakout action stars that can carry a major film ??! ! (Should we be maybe checking out goofy coming of age dramatic Netflix or Amazon comedies ala modern-day versions of Risky Business to find the next Tom Cruise? Can we find the next young, smart mouthed reluctant action hero like Bruce Willis? Is there a young underdog character that we can root that can do what Stallone did for Rocky I,II & III? Will we ever give young Wesley Snipes (Blade) and young Michael Jai White (Spawn) the credit they deserve for keeping the live action comicbook based film industry alive during the 90s before 20th Century Fox's first attempt at MCU  arrived with their Spider-Man, mediocre Fantastic Four or X-Men films?

textDisplay: Imagine if Marvel made an Iron Monger movie without Iron Man, a Red Skull movie without Captain America, an Abomination movie without the Hulk, a Baron Zemo movie without an Avengers team to split apart. Then imagine all these movies culminate in absolutely nothing because Marvel signed away the rights to the heroes that actually make the whole thing worthwhile.
textOriginal: Imagine if Marvel made an Iron Monger movie without Iron Man, a Red Skull movie without Captain America, an Abomination movie without the Hulk, a Baron Zemo movie without an Avengers team to split apart. Then imagine all these movies culminate in absolutely nothing because Marvel signed away the rights to the heroes that actually make the whole thing worthwhile.

textDisplay: Imagine how many of these villains really started before the heroes did. You know, as much as anyone would love to see movies go 100% based off comics and source material, we’re basically getting exactly the same thing all the time. If Walt Disney did that with his films before he died, yeah it’d be something unique that he could achieve. But instead he gave us something different and fascinating. Or how Marvel Films started off, most of their films followed about 75% of the comics. The rest was made up. Time changes and so do the people working on this. If you know the old saying, “if you want something done right, you do it yourself”, then get yourself a way into Marvel’s writing and directing and make these films how you want them.
textOriginal: Imagine how many of these villains really started before the heroes did. You know, as much as anyone would love to see movies go 100% based off comics and source material, we’re basically getting exactly the same thing all the time. If Walt Disney did that with his films before he died, yeah it’d be something unique that he could achieve. But instead he gave us something different and fascinating. Or how Marvel Films started off, most of their films followed about 75% of the comics. The rest was made up. Time changes and so do the people working on this. If you know the old saying, “if you want something done right, you do it yourself”, then get yourself a way into Marvel’s writing and directing and make these films how you want them.

textDisplay:
So as where madam webb was actually great, morbius was one of the worst overall films ever. Kraven isn't even worth keeping as a character. Here's why Kraven will fail and get review bombed EVEN on streaming platforms. 

Has absolutely zero powers, none. Nothing at all.
 His gimmik is that he's a animal poacher. 
A rated R villain will NEVER work with spiderman, maybe spiderman noir though. Maybe. 
This films looks like a psycho child directed it. 
They wasted the genetic rhino on this character no one actually likes when he should have been reserved for spiderman noir. 

I look forward to sony learning their lesson from this trash. Any success is going to be a lie.

Examples of horrible movies "being successful" are the worst batman movie ever made with a extremely unrealistic year 2 batman  "the batman" whose villain wore a extremely offensive gimp suit. 
Another example is "the joker" which depicted a random criminal convicted for hurting kids in "that horrible unspeakable way" 
Then you have most horror movies, like saw, which realistically wouldn't ever gave been allowed in theaters or had success ever.

textDisplay: Carnage is actually a PG- 13 created criminal mastermind antagonist. I have seen Carnage is graphic comic books like that. Violence to that degree is not good for your eyes and mind. Cletus Kasidy / Carnage received psychological treatment first his psychotic behavior. He is the way he was because of abuse He suffered as a child from his parents, and grandparents. He is like Marvel Comics version of Freddy Kruegar from Wes Craven's 1984 " Nightmare On Elm Street" film series. Freddy Kruegar is very much like Cletus Kasidy / Carnage from a supernatural perspective.

textDisplay:
Johnny Cash has a powerful presence in movie trailers, and his music is often used to create an intense and emotional atmosphere. The soundtrack for "Logan" featured the iconic "Hurt," which perfectly matched the melancholic and somber tone of the film. In "Pirates of the Caribbean: Dead Men Tell no Tales," "Ain't No Grave" was used in the trailer, bringing a sense of fatalism and resilience.

Cash's deep voice and introspective lyrics evoke a wide range of emotions, from sadness to determination, making his music a powerful choice for movie trailers that aim to capture the audience's attention. It's fantastic to see how his legacy continues to live on through these uses.

In "Pirates of the Caribbean: Dead Men Tell no Tales," "Ain't No Grave" was used in the trailer, bringing a sense of fatalism and resilience.

Cash's deep voice and introspective lyrics evoke a wide range of emotions, from sadness to determination, making his music a powerful choice for movie trailers that aim to capture the audience's attention. It's fantastic to see how his legacy continues to live on through these uses.

textDisplay:
I am curious. On a side note... I love the Rhino in the comics. He can be funny, ridiculous, dramatic (his attempt at redemption, coupled with the fate of his wife). But a criminal genius ? I'm not sure that's a good recipe. Having said that, we haven't yet had a good recipe for Spider-man enemies WITHOUT Spidey from Sony. But if we ever get one, Kraven is, potentially, a good choice, especially if you play with his death wish ideas. I suppose they'll give them to Father Russell Crow. And that his son will grant it. 
And if that sucks, we'll always have the comic book ‘Kraven's Last Hunt’ and the Spider-Man 2 game.

textDisplay:
This could be good, and I feel like, if the trailer is any indication, they captured a compelling vibe for the character.  But I would have said the same for Morbius.  I think they really did a certain thing right for both films, but the problem is, these have (basically) never been titular characters.  It doesn't mean people aren't interested in them, but I think people want to see them in Spider-Man.

With the Batman movies, they didn't just jump straight into a Joker film.  We saw like 15 incarnations of the Joker on the Silver screen, including one that looked like an act that could never be followed, before the Joker got his own film.  So they got the investment of the crowd first, then sold the solo outing.

With Sony & the spider-verse villains, it's like they're trying to do what Marvel did with Thor, Captain America, and Iron Man in doing solo outings first.  But those were all titular characters who already had mainline fans, had their own comic book titles for years, already appeared as video game characters and myriad action figures, etc.  

So with Sony and the spider-verse villains, what they really needed to do was present them with Spidey, first and foremost, and showcase them as a compelling character there, and then run with it solo if the presence is well received in the first place.  To do that, they'd need to make a spider-man film that neither has 3 or more spider-people, nor 3 or more spider-villains, because there's such a thing as too much?  But I mean, they could do it.  If you want to elevate a B-sides character, showcase them in a supporting role and impress us.  

So again going back to the beginning to tie it up, I think they captured a good vibe here, but I'm concerned that they didn't bother establishing "who cares?" with the audience before thrusting this character into the spotlight.
textOriginal:
This could be good, and I feel like, if the trailer is any indication, they captured a compelling vibe for the character.  But I would have said the same for Morbius.  I think they really did a certain thing right for both films, but the problem is, these have (basically) never been titular characters.  It doesn't mean people aren't interested in them, but I think people want to see them in Spider-Man.

With the Batman movies, they didn't just jump straight into a Joker film.  We saw like 15 incarnations of the Joker on the Silver screen, including one that looked like an act that could never be followed, before the Joker got his own film.  So they got the investment of the crowd first, then sold the solo outing.

With Sony & the spider-verse villains, it's like they're trying to do what Marvel did with Thor, Captain America, and Iron Man in doing solo outings first.  But those were all titular characters who already had mainline fans, had their own comic book titles for years, already appeared as video game characters and myriad action figures, etc.  

So with Sony and the spider-verse villains, what they really needed to do was present them with Spidey, first and foremost, and showcase them as a compelling character there, and then run with it solo if the presence is well received in the first place.  To do that, they'd need to make a spider-man film that neither has 3 or more spider-people, nor 3 or more spider-villains, because there's such a thing as too much?  But I mean, they could do it.  If you want to elevate a B-sides character, showcase them in a supporting role and impress us.  

So again going back to the beginning to tie it up, I think they captured a good vibe here, but I'm concerned that they didn't bother establishing "who cares?" with the audience before thrusting this character into the spotlight.

*Pietro Maximoff

textDisplay:
Well in all honesty these are villains, except rhino mostly, who operate outside of New York. 
They were going to make a movie about silver sable and black cat who also don't spend all their time in New York. 
In comics, more so recent runs, venom operates outside of New York. 
So while the MCU has an agreement to use peter Parker, I think the Sony verse is just focusing on more international villains and anti heroes so they don't have to place them all in New York.

textDisplay: Sony needs to stop making Spider-Man movies without Spider-Man. In the comics and animated series, Morbius worked because Spider-Man (and Blade) were the only heroes strong enough to battle Morbius. Kraven worked because he wanted to kill the ultimate prey in Spider-Man. Venom worked because he is anti-Spider-Man. How tf do they continue a Spider-Man universe of characters without a Spider-Man.....?
textOriginal: Sony needs to stop making Spider-Man movies without Spider-Man. In the comics and animated series, Morbius worked because Spider-Man (and Blade) were the only heroes strong enough to battle Morbius. Ryan Reynolds - hannibal king, GL, DP

textDisplay: A writer I know told me about a better script than this one. Sergei is a teenager who lives in his mansion with his parents and half-brother. He develops a passion for hunting. One day, Dimitri leaves and is recruited by Gustav Fiers. While still a teenager, Sergei's parents pass away. In his early adulthood, he decides to use his fortune to travel across the 9 continents hunting the largest predators. Tired of using firearms, he decides to hunt using only bladed weapons. This is not enough. He looks for a woman named Calypso, who gives him a potion that gives him super abilities. Calypso is immediately attracted to Kraven and they get involved. Kraven hunts his prey, but dissatisfied and wanting to be the best in the world, he uses his money to hire the best hunters to hunt him. Kraven is the prey this time. He thinks he killed all the hunters, but one survived and sought revenge. The final battle takes place on the outskirts of Kraven's mansion and after he wins, he becomes the greatest hunter in the world.
textOriginal: A writer I know told me about a better script than this one. Sergei is a teenager who lives in his mansion with his parents and half-brother. He develops a passion for hunting. One day, Dimitri leaves and is recruited by Gustav Fiers. While still a teenager, Sergei's parents pass away. In his early adulthood, he decides to use his fortune to travel across the 9 continents hunting the largest predators. Tired of using firearms, he decides to hunt using only bladed weapons. This is not enough. He looks for a woman named Calypso, who gives him a potion that gives him super abilities. Calypso is immediately attracted to Kraven and they get involved. Kraven hunts his prey, but dissatisfied and wanting to be the best in the world, he uses his money to hire the best hunters to hunt him. Kraven is the prey this time. He thinks he killed all the hunters, but one survived and sought revenge. The final battle takes place on the outskirts of Kraven's mansion and after he wins, he becomes the greatest hunter in the world.

textOriginal: Official Marvel channel does not usually upload Sony films except Spiderman films. Does this mean Kraven is part of MCU?

textDisplay: no, it's not in the MCU. Marvel are contractually obligated to promote the Sony Marvel movies as part of the agreement for the MCU to have access to the Spider-Man movie and TV rights. the reason this one is being promoted here is because Kraven is a Spider-Man villain, making this *TECHNICALLY* (but not really) a Spider-Man movie.
textOriginal: no, it's not in the MCU. Marvel are contractually obligated to promote the Sony Marvel movies as part of the agreement for the MCU to have access to the Spider-Man movie and TV rights. the reason this one is being promoted here is because Kraven is a Spider-Man villain, making this *TECHNICALLY* (but not really) a Spider-Man movie.

textDisplay:
La violence fait quand-même "faux", tout comme le scénario - qui espère héroïser un personnage fondamentalement mauvais, avant même qu'il ait fait l'expérience d'une vie de crime.
S'il y avait quelques extraits de combats antérieurs avec Spider-Man (une bonne doublure dans le costume du Tisseur et c'est bon), là ça serait plus cohérent.
Étrange aussi cette adaptation plus "intello" de Sytsevich - après le gros lourdaud joué par Giamatti, au moins on ne bégaye pas.
Et vive ses bons vieux accents russes ! Comme si on était revenu dans les vieux films d'action d'antan et f#€k la fidélité !
textOriginal:
La violence fait quand-même "faux", tout comme le scénario - qui espère héroïser un personnage fondamentalement mauvais, avant même qu'il ait fait l'expérience d'une vie de crime.
S'il y avait quelques extraits de combats antérieurs avec Spider-Man (une bonne doublure dans le costume du Tisseur et c'est bon), là ça serait plus cohérent.
Étrange aussi cette adaptation plus "intello" de Sytsevich - après le gros lourdaud joué par Giamatti, au moins on ne bégaye pas.
Et vive ses bons vieux accents russes ! Comme si on était revenu dans les vieux films d'action d'antan et f#€k la fidélité !
